note: Do not instantiate directly; use itasca.attach module functions.

methods.name: attach_delete
methods.description: Deletes an attachment.

methods.name: extra
methods.description: Get the attach extra data in the given slot.

methods.name: follower
methods.description: Get the follower gridpoint.

methods.name: group
methods.description: Get the attach group name in a given slot.

methods.name: group_remove
methods.signature: attach.group_remove(group_name: str or int[, slot: str or int]) -> bool
methods.description: Remove from the given group from all group slots of the attach. One argument of type string, giving the group name, is required. The return value is a bool which is True if the group was removed from any slot, otherwise False.

methods.name: groups
methods.description: Get a dictionary describing which groups this attach is part of. The keys of the dictionary are the slot names and the values are the group names.

methods.name: id
methods.description: Get the attach id.

methods.name: in_group
methods.signature: attach.in_group(group_name: str or int[, slot: str or int]) -> bool
methods.description: Test if the attach is part of a given group. If the optional argument slot is given, only that slot is searched. Otherwise, all group slots are searched.

methods.name: leader
methods.description: In 3D, this function returns a tuple of 1, 2 or 4 gridpoint objects for gridpoint, edge or face attach types. In 2D, this function returns a tuple of 1 or 2 gridpoint objects for gridpoint or face attach types.

methods.name: leader_face
methods.description: Get the face index associated with this attach condition (from 0 to 5 in 3D and 0 to 3 in 2D), or None if this is not a face attach.

methods.name: leader_zone
methods.description: Get a pointer to the leader zone for face attachments. If the attachment type is not a face, the function returns None.

methods.name: pos
methods.description: Get the attach point location.

methods.name: set_extra
methods.description: Set the attach extra data in the given slot.

methods.name: set_group
methods.signature: attach.set_group(group_name: str or int[, slot: str or int]) -> None
methods.description: Set the attach group name in a given slot.

methods.name: set_snap
methods.description: Det the attach point snap option. Gridpoints on opposite attached faces will maintain their position if snap is True. Snap is True by default.

methods.name: set_weight_edge
methods.description: (3D ONLY) Set a value in the range 0.0 to 1.0 which describes the location of the gridpoint in the edge attach. If the attachment type is not edge, the function raises a ValueError.

methods.name: set_weight_face
methods.description: (3D ONLY) Set the face weight components with the vector value weight. The indiviual values must be in the range 0 to 1.0 and must sum to less that 1.0. The value corresponds to the location of the gridpoints in the face with respect to the face vertices; for face attachments. The fourth quad weight can be computed by subtracting all three weights on the vector from 1.0. If the attachment is not of face type a ValueError is raised.

methods.name: snap
methods.description: Get the attach point snap option. Gridpoints on opposite attached faces will maintain their position if snap is True. Snap is True by default.

methods.name: type
methods.description: A value of "gridpoint", "edge" (in 3D only), or "face" depending on the attach type.

methods.name: valid
methods.description: Returns True if this attach is live.

methods.name: weight_edge
methods.description: (3D ONLY) Get a value in the range 0.0 to 1.0 which describes the location of the gridpoint in the edge attach. If the attachment type is not edge, the function returns None.

methods.name: weight_face
methods.description: (3D ONLY) Get a vector with all components in the range 0 to 1.0. The value corresponds to the location of the gridpoints in the face with respect to the face vertices; for face attachments. The fourth quad weight can be computed by subtracting all three weights on the vector from 1.0. If the attachment is not of face type None is returned.
